notes: Switched-fabric I/O interconnect standard; became dominant in HPC/AI clusters; RDMA concepts influenced RoCE and NVMe-oF.
Dominant server I/O bus standard at time of writing; 133 MB/s shared bus bandwidth.
Extended PCI with higher bandwidth; superseded by PCI Express (PCIe); cited as inadequate for long-term data center needs.
Serial replacement for PCI bus touted for 2004; became the universal server I/O standard.
High-performance storage networking protocol; FC SANs widely deployed in large enterprise data centers.
Ubiquitous parallel server-to-storage connectivity protocol; superseded by SAS and NVMe.
SCSI over TCP/IP; cited as an emerging storage networking trend alongside IBA.
File-level storage over Ethernet/IP; cost-effective alternative to SAN cited in the paper.
High-speed storage networking fabric; FC-based SANs were primary enterprise storage at time of writing.
Dominant networking protocol; IBA carried IP as native network layer for WAN connectivity.
IBA adapter in server connecting to IBA fabric; offloads I/O processing from CPU.
IBA adapter embedded in storage arrays and I/O devices connecting to IBA fabric.
High-density server form factor; IBA cited as solving bandwidth/cabling constraints for blade racks.
Core IBA capability enabling zero-copy data transfer; influenced RoCE and NVMe-oF in modern AI/ML infrastructure.
